title: Beijing
subtitle: Beijing · 北京 · 5 routes
meta: China · red gates, grey roofs, hutong lakes, and the Wall beyond the hills
practicalNote: Beijing is easiest to read in large layers: the axis and gates first, then low hutongs by water, evening food streets, and a separate day outside the city on the Wall. Straight lines hide real distance here, so a good day follows one clear route rather than trying to collect every symbol at once. Official bookings and documents matter for the palace and square, but they should support the walk, not replace the city.

photo.alt: Пекинская ось на рассвете: серые крыши, красная стена и дальние горы

identity.hook: An axis of gates, roofs, and the northern hill

title: Seoul
subtitle: Seoul · 서울 · 5 routes
meta: South Korea - palaces, hills, markets, museums, and the Hangang
practicalNote: April-May and September-October are usually easier for long walks. In summer, plan around heat, heavy rain, and typhoon season; in winter, expect cold, and check forecast plus fine dust before outdoor routes. Use metro or taxi between the Hangang, Yongsan, Namsan, and the old center: walking distances stretch quickly on the map.

photo.alt: Сеул у реки Ханган с силуэтом Намсана и крышами внизу

title: Belgrade
subtitle: Belgrade · Beograd · 6 routes
meta: Serbia - fortress, rivers, Zemun, and modernism
practicalNote: City buses, trams, trolleybuses, and BG Train have been free since 2025. Keep dinars for small purchases and markets; in Belgrade you will see both Cyrillic and Latin scripts.

identity.hook: Fortress above Roman Singidunum - 1st c. CE

title: Sarajevo
subtitle: Sarajevo · 5 routes
meta: Bosnia and Herzegovina - old bazaar, river, museums, and mountains
practicalNote: Central walks are short, but cobblestones and slopes get tiring after rain. For Ilidza, Trebevic, and Tunnel of Hope, check transport, cable-car operation, and hours on the day; keep some KM cash. Outside the city, stay on paved or marked routes because of landmine and unexploded-ordnance risk.

photo.alt: Sebilj fountain on Bascarsija square in Sarajevo

title: Tivat
subtitle: Tivat · 5 routes
meta: Montenegro - bay, marina, salt flats, and Vrmac slopes
practicalNote: In summer, the waterfront and Solila heat up fast: keep exposed routes for morning or evening. Check buses, beach access, and museum hours on the day you go because several places change by season. Keep some euro cash for small payments even though cards usually work around the marina.

photo.alt: Тиватская бухта с мариной, набережной и зелеными склонами
